articles.title: Hong Kong must wake up to the cold hard geopolitics of AI
articles.summary: Hong Kong is at a critical juncture as the geopolitics of artificial intelligence begin to dictate access to essential technologies, impacting major financial institutions and highlighting the urgent need for local officials to grasp the real-world implications of AI in their city. This shift not only redefines competitive dynamics but also raises existential questions about the future of innovation and economic stability in the region.

articles.title: More Americans think China is world leader in AI, new survey reveals
articles.summary: A recent Pew Research Centre survey reveals that over one-third of Americans now view China as the leading nation in artificial intelligence, a significant shift that underscores growing concerns about the U.S.'s competitive edge in this critical technology sector. This perception could influence diplomatic discussions, particularly as AI becomes a focal point in upcoming U.S.-China relations.

articles.title: GeForce NOW Sets Sail With ‘Path of Exile: Curse of the Allflame’ Joining the Cloud
articles.summary: NVIDIA's GeForce NOW is revolutionizing cloud gaming by enabling instant access to new titles like 'Path of Exile: Curse of the Allflame' and major updates for Battlefield 6, eliminating download times and enhancing the gaming experience for users. This breakthrough not only expands the library of accessible games but also underscores the growing significance of cloud technology in delivering seamless entertainment.

articles.title: Apple Maps to power navigation experience for Ford UEV Platform
articles.summary: Apple's integration of Apple Maps into Ford's UEV Platform marks a significant leap in automotive navigation, enhancing user experience with seamless, real-time mapping capabilities that could redefine how drivers interact with their vehicles. This collaboration not only elevates Ford's electric vehicle offerings but also positions Apple as a key player in the automotive tech landscape.

articles.title: Global AI experts push back on US ‘distillation’ claims against Moonshot’s Kimi K3 model
articles.summary: Global AI experts are challenging the Trump administration's allegations that Moonshot AI's Kimi K3 model improperly distilled US models, asserting that such practices do not equate to intellectual property theft and highlighting the political motivations behind the accusations. This pushback underscores the ongoing debate over AI ownership and the implications for innovation in the tech industry.

articles.title: US eyes ban on Chinese humanoid robots as US-China tech rivalry intensifies
articles.summary: The US is taking a significant step in its tech rivalry with China by advancing legislation that bans the military from using Chinese-made humanoid robots, highlighting escalating concerns over national security and data privacy in the realm of autonomous systems. This move underscores the growing urgency for the US to safeguard its technological landscape amidst increasing competition with China.

articles.title: All the Queens houses
articles.summary:
• The diverse architectural styles of Queens' homes reflect a rich tapestry of cultural influences, showcasing the neighborhood's global identity.  
• Each residence serves as a canvas, revealing the personal narratives and dreams of its inhabitants through unique design choices.  
• The juxtaposition of various cultural motifs invites reflection on the interconnectedness of communities in a modern urban landscape.  
• These homes stand as testaments to the idea that identity is not static but rather a fluid interplay of heritage and personal expression.

articles.title: China’s tech firms turn the global AI boom into opportunity amid fierce rivalry at home
articles.summary: Chinese tech firms are seizing the global AI boom by expanding their international presence through advanced cloud-based software and foundation models, capitalizing on a dramatic increase in tech exports that reflects their competitive edge in a rapidly evolving market. This strategic shift not only enhances their global influence but also positions them as key players in the future of AI technology.

articles.title: The savage bites back
articles.summary:
• Anthropophagy, as a cultural practice, reclaims and reinterprets colonial narratives through the act of consumption and transformation.  
• This Brazilian modernist movement challenges the dichotomy of civilization versus savagery, revealing the complexities of identity and power.  
• By embracing cannibalism metaphorically, societies can digest and repurpose foreign influences, fostering a unique cultural synthesis.  
• The act of 'biting back' symbolizes resistance against colonial oppression, turning historical trauma into a source of strength and creativity.  
• Ultimately, anthropophagy invites a deeper reflection on how cultures evolve through the interplay of dominance and adaptation.

articles.title: China’s new chip stores data with a single electron, breaking AI memory bottleneck
articles.summary: A groundbreaking development from Fudan University has achieved a monumental leap in data storage technology by enabling chips to store information using just a single electron, potentially revolutionizing AI memory systems and overcoming the longstanding limitations of traditional DRAM, which requires around 200,000 electrons per bit. This breakthrough not only enhances efficiency but also paves the way for more powerful and compact computing solutions in the future.
